name: tinyhtml5

metadata.name: tinyhtml5
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: HTML parser based on the WHATWG HTML specification

documentation:
**A tiny HTML5 parser**

tinyhtml5 is a HTML5 parser that transforms a possibly malformed HTML document
into an ElementTree tree.

This module is a simplified fork of html5lib, written and maintained by James
Graham, Sam Sneddon, Łukasz Langa and Will Kahn-Greene.

* Free software: MIT license
* For Python 3.9+, tested on CPython and PyPy
* Documentation: https://doc.courtbouillon.org/tinyhtml5
* Changelog: https://github.com/CourtBouillon/tinyhtml5/releases
* Code, issues, tests: https://github.com/CourtBouillon/tinyhtml5
* Code of conduct: https://www.courtbouillon.org/code-of-conduct
* Professional support: https://www.courtbouillon.org
* Donation: https://opencollective.com/courtbouillon

Copyrights are retained by their contributors, no copyright assignment is
required to contribute to tinyhtml5. Unless explicitly stated otherwise, any
contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion is licensed under the MIT
license, without any additional terms or conditions. For full authorship
information, see the version control history.

modules.name: tinyhtml5
documentation:
HTML parsing library based on the WHATWG HTML specification.

The parser is designed to be compatible with existing HTML found in the wild
and implements well-defined error recovery that is largely compatible with
modern desktop web browsers.

Example usage:

```
import tinyhtml5
tree = tinyhtml5.parse("/path/to/document.html")
```

modules.name: tinyhtml5.treebuilder
documentation: Tree builder.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.treebuilder.ActiveFormattingElements.append
documentation: Append node to the end of the list.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.treebuilder.Element.has_content
documentation: Return True if the node has children or text, False otherwise.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.treebuilder.Element.append_child
documentation: Insert node as a child of the current node.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.treebuilder.Element.insert_before
documentation:
Insert node as a child of the current node, before reference.

Raise ValueError if reference is not a child of the current node.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.treebuilder.Element.remove_child
documentation: Remove node from the children of the current node.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.treebuilder.Element.insert_text
documentation:
Insert data as text in the current node.

Text is positioned before the start of node insert_before or to the end
of the node's text.

If insert_before is a node, insert the text before this node.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.treebuilder.Element.clone
documentation:
Return a shallow copy of the current node.

The node has the same name and attributes, but no parent or children.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.treebuilder.Element.reparent_children
documentation:
Move all the children of the current node to parent.

This is needed so that trees that don't store text as nodes move the
text in the correct way.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.treebuilder.TreeBuilder.__init__
documentation:
Create a TreeBuilder.

If namespace_html_elements is True, namespace HTML elements.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.treebuilder.TreeBuilder.element_in_active_formatting_elements
documentation:
Find name between end of active formatting elements and last marker.

If an element with this name exists, return it. Else return False.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.treebuilder.TreeBuilder.create_element

methods.name: tinyhtml5.treebuilder.TreeBuilder.insert_text

methods.name: tinyhtml5.treebuilder.TreeBuilder.get_table_misnested_node_position
documentation: Get foster parent element and sibling (or None) to insert before.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.treebuilder.TreeBuilder.get_document

Tree builder.

functions.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.lookup_encoding
documentation:
Return the Python codec name corresponding to an encoding.

Return None if the string doesn't correspond to a valid encoding.

classes.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.HTMLUnicodeInputStream

methods.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.HTMLUnicodeInputStream.__init__
documentation:
Initialise the HTMLInputStream.

Create a normalized stream from source for use by tinyhtml5.

source can be either a file-object, local filename or a string.

The optional encoding parameter must be a string that indicates
the encoding.  If specified, that encoding will be used,
regardless of any BOM or later declaration (such as in a meta
element).

methods.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.HTMLUnicodeInputStream.open_stream
documentation:
Produce a file object from source.

source can be either a file object, local filename or a string.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.HTMLUnicodeInputStream.position
documentation: Return (line, col) of the current position in the stream.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.HTMLUnicodeInputStream.character
documentation:
Read one character from the stream or queue if available.

Return EOF when EOF is reached.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.HTMLUnicodeInputStream.chars_until
documentation:
Return a string of characters from the stream.

String goes up to but does not include any character in 'characters' or
EOF. 'characters' must be a container that supports the 'in' method and
iteration over its characters.

Provides a Unicode stream of characters to the HTMLTokenizer.

This class takes care of character encoding and removing or replacing
incorrect byte-sequences and also provides column and line tracking.

classes.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.HTMLBinaryInputStream

methods.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.HTMLBinaryInputStream.detect_bom
documentation:
Attempt to detect at BOM at the start of the stream.

If an encoding can be determined from the BOM return the name of the
encoding otherwise return None.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.HTMLBinaryInputStream.detect_encoding_meta
documentation: Report the encoding declared by the meta element.

Provide a binary stream of characters to the HTMLTokenizer.

This class takes care of character encoding and removing or replacing
incorrect byte-sequences and also provides column and line tracking.

classes.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.EncodingBytes

methods.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.EncodingBytes.skip
documentation: Skip past a list of characters.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.EncodingBytes.match_bytes
documentation:
Look for a sequence of bytes at the start of a string.

If the bytes are found return True and advance the position to the byte
after the match. Otherwise return False and leave the position alone.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.EncodingBytes.jump_to
documentation:
Look for the next sequence of bytes matching a given sequence.

If a match is found advance the position to the last byte of the match.

Bytes-like object with an associated position and various extra methods.

If the position is ever greater than the string length then an exception is
raised.

classes.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.EncodingParser

methods.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.EncodingParser.handle_comment
documentation: Skip over comments.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.inputstream.EncodingParser.get_attribute
documentation:
Return a (name, value) pair for the next attribute in the stream.

If no attribute is found, return None.
Mini parser for detecting character encoding from meta elements.

classes.name: tinyhtml5.tokenizer.HTMLTokenizer

methods.name: tinyhtml5.tokenizer.HTMLTokenizer.__iter__
documentation:
This is where the magic happens.

We do our usually processing through the states and when we have a token
to return we yield the token which pauses processing until the next token
is requested.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.tokenizer.HTMLTokenizer.parse_error
documentation: Add a parse error to the token queue.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.tokenizer.HTMLTokenizer.characters
documentation: Add a characters string to the token queue.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.tokenizer.HTMLTokenizer.consume_number_entity
documentation:
Return either U+FFFD or the character based on the representation.

It also discards ";" if present. If not present self.parse_error is
invoked.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.tokenizer.HTMLTokenizer.process_entity_in_attribute
documentation: Replace the need for entity_in_attribute_value_state.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.tokenizer.HTMLTokenizer.emit_current_token
documentation:
This method is a generic handler for emitting the tags.

It also sets the state to "data" because that's what's needed after a
token has been emitted.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.tokenizer.HTMLTokenizer.data_state
documentation: Below are the various tokenizer states worked out.
HTML tokenizer.

functions.name: tinyhtml5.parser.parse
documentation:
Parse an HTML document into a tree. ``` param document The document to parse as a HTML string, filename, file-like object. type document , , or param bool namespace_html_elements Whether or not to namespace HTML elements. ``` Extra parameters can be provided to define possible encodings if the document is given as {class}`bytes`. ``` param override_encoding Forced encoding provided by user agent. type override_encoding str or bytes param transport_encoding Encoding provided by transport layout. type transport_encoding str or bytes param same_origin_parent_encoding Parent document encoding. type same_origin_parent_encoding str or bytes param likely_encoding Possible encoding provided by user agent. type likely_encoding str or bytes param default_encoding Encoding used as fallback. type default_encoding str or bytes returns . ``` Example: ```pycon >>> from tinyhtml5 import parse >>> parse('

This is a doc

') ```

classes.name: tinyhtml5.parser.HTMLParser

methods.name: tinyhtml5.parser.HTMLParser.encoding
documentation:
Name of the character encoding that was used to decode the input stream.

{obj}`None` if that is not determined yet.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.parser.HTMLParser.parse
documentation:
Parse a HTML document into a well-formed tree.

If `full_tree` is `True`, return the whole tree.

methods.name: tinyhtml5.parser.HTMLParser.parse_fragment
documentation:
Parse a HTML fragment into a well-formed tree fragment.

`container` is the tag name of the fragment’s container.

HTML parser.

Generate a tree structure from a stream of (possibly malformed) HTML.

documentation: the real thing

methods.name: tinyhtml5.parser.InBodyPhase.process_eof

methods.name: tinyhtml5.parser.InBodyPhase.start_tag_misplaced
documentation:
Elements that should be children of other elements.

Here they are ignored: "caption", "col", "colgroup", "frame",
"frameset", "head", "option", "optgroup", "tbody", "td", "tfoot",
"th", "thead", "tr", "noscript".

methods.name: tinyhtml5.parser.InBodyPhase.end_tag_formatting
documentation: The much-feared adoption agency algorithm.
